name: joint-workflow
description: Joint workflows define discontinuity geometry or joint sets, then connect those definitions to zone/interface behavior. In FLAC, joint-related commands are part of the zone/fracture command families.

notes: Use geometry for explicit surfaces and fracture/joint-set commands for statistical or set-based discontinuities.
Separating or interfacing zones changes connectivity and should be checked before solving.

commands: zone interface node property cohesion 0 friction 28 tension 0
notes: Joint behavior often depends more on normal/shear stiffness and strength than on geometry alone.

validation_notes: Plot groups and interfaces after generating joints.
Check that discontinuities cross the expected zones.
Use histories or FISH to monitor slip/opening at representative locations.
